page: Microsoft Azure E16_v5 is an Ev5-family memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz, the server features 8 physical cores with hyper-threading supporting 2 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

This server type does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via remote attached volumes. Additionally, there are no integrated GPUs, which limits its utility for hardware-accelerated graphics or heavy parallel computing tasks. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core positions this instance as a resource-dense option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local temporary storage or GPU acceleration.

Benchmark evaluations place the E16_v5 in the average performance tier across most metrics. It achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1635.0 and a multi-core score of 9891.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 21149.34. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 833.81 GB/sec and an uncached large block write speed of 53.92 GB/sec. In database and transactional tests, the server processed 15.85 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 8005.49 in PassMark database operations. For language model tasks, the server demonstrated strong performance in text generation using a small 135M model at 279.0 tokens per second, while medium 7B model generation operated at an average speed of 12.21 tokens per second.

Given its 128.0 GB memory capacity and dedicated Intel Xeon processing cores, the E16_v5 is configured for memory-intensive applications. Suitable workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio without the need for local scratch disks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E16_v5 is an Ev5-family memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading. The hardware configuration lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on remote storage. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most operations, with a PassMark CPU score of 21149.34 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.85 million operations per second. It demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 279.0 tokens per second. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks that do not require local temporary storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16_v5 is an Ev5-family memory-optimized server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced database and in-memory caching performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for database workloads.
tagline: An x86 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Average performance in database operations and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Strong text generation speed of 279.0 tokens per second for small LLM models
Configured for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data processing
